name: Cave (68000-based)
notes: Public core by furrtek (MiSTer-devel). Chisel-written. 8 games public. 7 more unimplemented including Air Gallet, Gogetsuji Legends, Power Instinct 2, Sailor Moon. Could contribute missing game support rather than building from scratch.

name: Cave CV1000 (SH-3 based)
notes: SH-3 CPU is extremely wide (32-bit superscalar-like) and would consume ~80K ALMs alone. Custom sprite ASIC on top makes this infeasible. Beyond DE-10 Nano budget. Would need FPGA + HPS hybrid approach.
skip_reason: CPU alone exceeds DE-10 Nano budget

name: Konami System GX
notes: Genuine gap — no public core. 68020 alone is ~45K ALMs (we have chips/m68020). Custom GFX chips 056832/055555 are extremely complex. GX includes a K056832 tilemap chip and K055555 priority blender — Konami's most complex 2D sprite system. Very tight on DE-10 Nano.
skip_reason: 68020 + 6 custom chips is at DE-10 Nano limit

name: Jaleco Mega System 1 (Type B/C)
notes: Public beta: va7deo/MegaSys1_A (14 games). Types B/C (Desert Assault, Earth Defense Force) untouched. Type B adds 3 scroll layers, Type C adds rotation chip. Good game library with Rodland, Desert Assault cult classics. Building on va7deo's Type A work makes B/C incremental.

name: SETA 1 / SETA 2
notes: Genuine gap — no public core. SETA 1 uses custom X1-001A/X1-002A sprite chips, relatively well-documented in MAME (seta.cpp). No FPGA work known. 68000-based so CPU is solved. SETA 2 uses V60 + different customs — harder. Good game library. Thundercade, Twin Eagle, Grindstormer are desirable. Moderate complexity.

name: Technos / WWF Wrestlefest hardware
notes: Public: technos16/technosc via Coin-Op Collection. Technos later hardware (WrestleFest, Combatribes) evolved from Double Dragon hardware — jotego already has DD, DD2, Kunio cores. WWF Wrestlefest is a high-demand game on MiSTer community.

name: SNK NeoGeo MVS/AES
notes: Excellent mature core. MVS, AES, CDZ all supported. Known limitation: encrypted ROMs not supported — requires decrypted MAME or Darksoft sets. 8 games need 128MB SDRAM module. Accuracy concern on encrypted variants.

name: Capcom CPS-1
notes: jotego cps1 core is mature and covers 80+ titles. Q-Sound is HLE-based (not cycle-accurate). Some games need 64MB SDRAM. Our CPS1 decompilation project is separate (native C port).

name: Capcom CPS-2
notes: jotego cps2 core covers 150+ titles. Encryption decrypted via keys. Some games need 64MB SDRAM module.

name: Data East (Karnov / Bad Dudes era)
notes: jotego has: ninja (Bad Dudes/Heavy Barrel), cop (RoboCop), midres (Midnight Resistance), slyspy. All mature. DECO 16 hardware family well-covered.

name: Data East DECO-32 / DE-156
notes: DE-156 chip is an encrypted ARM6 — requires key extraction or functional simulation. H6280 (PC Engine CPU) not yet in any jotego core. Custom DE-141/99 rendering pipeline poorly documented. DECO-32 is at the edge of DE-10 Nano feasibility due to ARM core + H6280 both needing implementation. Caveman Ninja and Fighter's History are high-demand games justifying the investment eventually.

name: Konami '88 / TMNT hardware (052109/051960)
notes: jotego aliens core covers TMNT, Crime Fighters, Gang Busters, Super Contra, Thunder Cross (14 games). Gradius III is in grad3 (schematics only, no HDL yet). This hardware family is well-covered.

name: Taito F2
notes: Community WIP by MiSTer-devel. 14 games implemented including Gun Frontier, Growl, Liquid Kids. Active development, no formal release yet. We have Taito chip infrastructure from B/F3/Z/X that may assist.

name: Irem M72 / M84
notes: Public: MiSTer-devel/Arcade-IremM72. Mature core with 12 games including R-Type, Ninja Spirit, Image Fight. V30 CPU is well-implemented. M84 covered as M72 derivative.

name: Irem M92
notes: Public: MiSTer-devel/Arcade-IremM92. Mature core with numerous games. In the Hunt and Ninja Baseball Batman are community favorites. 287 commits, active development.

name: Raizing / 8ing (Battle Garegga hardware)
notes: Exists (archived Feb 2025): psomashekar/Raizing_FPGA. Uses Toaplan V2 hardware (GP9001 ASIC) — we already have GP9001 implemented. Battle Garegga is widely considered one of the greatest shooters ever made and has extreme community demand. Raizing games share GP9001 and YMZ280B sound.

name: Mitchell / Capcom Pang hardware
notes: jotego pang core covers Pang, Super Pang, Block Block (3 games, allegedly 100% accurate). Mitchell Pang/Chariot/Magical Crystals on separate hardware that may overlap with Kaneko infrastructure — needs investigation.
key_chips: custom Mitchell sprite IC or shared Kaneko KC-001
skip_reason: Pang already in jotego; Mitchell variants need research

name: Toaplan Version 1
notes: va7deo has zerowing (4 games), vimana (2 games), demonswld (1 game), rallybike (1 game). jotego has rbike separately. Comprehensive coverage. This was our architecture reference for Toaplan V2.

name: Toaplan Version 2
notes: Also: active beta at atrac17/Toaplan2. Teki Paki, Tatsujin Oh, Whoopee!!, Snow Bros. 2 running. Batsugun, Dogyuun, Knuckle Bash, FixEight still WIP. We have our own GP9001 implementation. This is our active build target.
key_chips: GP9001 (custom sprite/tilemap ASIC) — we have this

name: NMK16
notes: Simplest custom-chip architecture in our build order. No custom GFX ICs — uses discrete logic. 68000 + Z80, YM2203/YM2151 + OKI6295. 10 titles. Best 'training project' for pipeline. We have the nmk_arcade and nmk directories.

name: Psikyo (SH-2 / PS2V1 hardware)
notes: SH-2 CPU is complex (~40K ALMs) but proven in some FPGA projects. 4 custom sprite/tilemap chips (PS2001B, PS3103, PS3204, PS3305). jotego has gunbird2 directory with schematics only. Psikyo is arguably the most desirable untouched 2D shmup hardware. Gunbird, Strikers 1945 series are apex cult classics. We have psikyo and psikyo_arcade directories.

name: Kaneko 16 / Super Nova System
notes: 7 custom chips but 68000-based which is solved. Main complexity is VU-002/VU-003 sprite/tile pipeline, KC-001/KC-002 sprite ICs, CALC3/TOYBOX MCU subsystems (can be stubbed for most games). We have kaneko and kaneko_arcade directories.

name: Seibu SPI System (Raiden 2 hardware)
notes: NOT FEASIBLE: i386 CPU, too large for DE-10 Nano. MiSTer Pocket only. Seibu SPI system uses an i386 main CPU (~200K+ ALMs) — well beyond the DE-10 Nano 110K ceiling. Raiden 2/DX are extremely high-demand but require a more capable FPGA platform.

name: Taito F3
notes: We have Taito F3 infrastructure in chips/taito_f3. 68EC020 is wider than 68000 but feasible. Key challenge: TC0630FDP sprite engine + TC0480SCP tilemap + ES5505 ENSONIQ sound. F3 game library is exceptional (Darius Gaiden, Elevator Action Returns). We have tc0630fdp, tc0480scp, es5506 directories.

name: Taito B System
notes: Already in our pipeline (chips/taito_b). TC0180VCU handles tiles. TC0140SYT for sound communication. Ninja Warriors (triple-screen) is iconic. We have tc0180vcu directory.

name: Taito Z System
notes: Already in our pipeline (chips/taito_z). Road generation (TC0150ROD) + sprite scaling (TC0370MSO). Chase HQ and Top Speed are iconic racing games. We have tc0150rod, tc0370mso directories.

name: Taito X System
notes: Already in our pipeline (chips/taito_x). Distinct from CPS-based Taito X hardware.

name: Sega Super Scaler (Space Harrier / OutRun / After Burner)
notes: Public: jtoutrun by jotego (mature). OutRun (all variants + Enhanced Edition), Turbo OutRun, Super Hang-On all covered. Space Harrier uses different hardware board (not OutRun hardware) — no dedicated core. After Burner II on X Board — untouched. The 'Super Scaler' name refers to the sprite-scaling chip shared across multiple Sega boards (OutRun, Space Harrier, X Board, Y Board).

name: Sega System 18
notes: Public: jts18 by jotego (Dec 2025). Shadow Dancer, Clutch Hitter, Alien Storm covered. Uses 68000 + Z80, shared Sega sprite/tile infrastructure with System 16.

name: Sega X Board (After Burner II / Thunder Blade)
notes: Genuine gap — ~65K ALMs, feasible. No public core exists. X Board uses 2x 68000 (main + sub CPU), the Sega Super Scaler chips (315-5011/5012) plus a 'Super Z80' sound system and custom rotation/scaling ASIC. After Burner II is extremely high-demand. Dual-CPU is achievable on DE-10 Nano (see Taito Z dual-68K). Main challenge is the scaling chip at sufficient speed.

name: Sega Y Board (G-LOC / Power Drift / Galaxy Force)
notes: More advanced than X Board. Uses 3 CPUs (2x 68000 + Z80) + polygon-like rotation hardware. Custom 315-5218/5219 rotation/scaling chips are the hardest part. Galaxy Force II and Power Drift are iconic. Tight on DE-10 Nano. Post X-Board target.

name: Sega System 16A/16B
notes: jotego s16/s16b cores are mature with 50+ games. FD1089/FD1094 encrypted games require correct key data.

name: Sega System 32
games: Golden Axe: The Revenge of Death Adder
notes: V60 (NEC 32-bit CISC) is complex — no known FPGA V60 implementation. Custom 315-5441 + 315-5442 GFX ASICs for sprite/tile. GA3 for multi-CPU coordination. Golden Axe: Revenge of Death Adder is extremely high-demand. V60 implementation would be a major new contribution to FPGA arcade.

name: Sega Model 1
notes: First Sega 3D hardware. TGP (Triangle Graphics Processor) is proprietary floating-point DSP. Two MB86233 chips for geometry + rendering. i960 main CPU. Virtua Fighter is historically iconic. NOT feasible on DE-10 Nano — would need a Cyclone 10 or higher. Moonshot target.
skip_reason: Custom DSP + 3D pipeline exceeds DE-10 Nano

name: Sega Model 2
notes: 4x SHARC DSPs doing floating-point 3D. WAY beyond DE-10 Nano (110K ALMs total). Requires dedicated high-end FPGA. Not a realistic target.
skip_reason: 4x SHARC DSPs massively exceed DE-10 Nano

name: Namco System 21 (Starblade / Winning Run)
notes: 5 TMS32025 DSPs doing 3D polygon rendering. Custom DSP code for polygon scanline filling. Starblade is iconic. Not feasible on DE-10 Nano — DSP array alone exceeds budget. Distant moonshot.
skip_reason: DSP array + 3D pipeline exceeds DE-10 Nano

name: Namco System 22 (Ridge Racer / Ace Combat)
notes: DSPs + texture mapping + complex 3D pipeline. Far beyond DE-10 Nano scope.
skip_reason: Complex 3D/texture engine far exceeds DE-10 Nano

name: Namco System I / System II (Galaga '88 hardware)
notes: jotego shouse core covers 30+ games including Galaga '88, Splatterhouse, Dragon Spirit, Pac-Mania. Mature.

name: Video System / Banpresto (Aero Fighters hardware)
notes: Genuine gap — no public core. Video System hardware is 68000-based with custom sprite/tile ICs. Aero Fighters (Sonic Wings in Japan) series has strong community demand. Simple architecture, well-documented in MAME (aerofgt.cpp). MAME quality excellent. Good beginner-to-intermediate project.

name: Taito H System
notes: Taito H uses TC0080VCO tilemap + sprites (unique to H). Small game library (3 games). Shares Taito audio infrastructure with B/F2 systems. Low priority due to small library but low difficulty makes it a candidate for a quick win.

name: Taito L System
notes: Taito L is Z80-based — extremely simple. TC0090LVC is a custom Z80 + GFX combo chip. Very small footprint. Plotting/Flipull and Puzznic are well-loved puzzle games. Easiest Taito system to implement. Quick win candidate.

name: Taito Air System (Top Landing / Air Inferno)
notes: Taito Air System is a 2.5D sprite-scaling system for flight simulators. TC0370MSO sprite scaler (same as Taito Z). Very small game library (2 games). Low priority despite interesting technology. Shares much with Taito Z.

name: Jaleco Mega System 32
notes: V70 is NEC's 32-bit CPU (like V60 but wider). No known FPGA V70 implementation. Custom 3D-ish effects (not true polygons — sprite zoom/rotation). Small game library. Very difficult due to V70 CPU + custom ASICs. Low priority vs difficulty.

name: Konami Gradius III (054986 hardware)
notes: jotego grad3 directory has schematics only, no HDL as of 2026. This hardware (053251 priority + 054986 sound + 052109/051960 tiles) is a superset of the already-mature aliens/tmnt hardware. High demand for Gradius III. Could contribute HDL to jotego grad3.

name: Technos WWF WrestleFest (later board)
notes: Public: technos16/technosc via Coin-Op Collection. WWF WrestleFest is the single most requested missing MiSTer arcade core per community polls. 68000-based, evolved from Double Dragon hardware. jotego has DD/DD2/Kunio.

name: Midway Y-Unit / T-Unit (Mortal Kombat)
notes: TMS34010 is a graphics-oriented 32-bit CPU with bit-BLT instructions. No known FPGA TMS34010 implementation. Mortal Kombat and NBA Jam are extremely high-demand. DCS audio system needs separate implementation. Complex but the TMS34010 is documented. Y-Unit uses DMA-based sprite blitting.

name: Atari System 1 (Marble Madness / Indiana Jones)
notes: WIP MiSTer core exists with 5 games. Graphical bugs in Indiana Jones cocktail mode. Could contribute fixes.

name: Sega ST-V (Saturn arcade)
notes: ST-V = Sega Saturn hardware in arcade form. Dual SH-2 + VDP1 + VDP2 graphics pipeline. Saturn console core is WIP in MiSTer community. Would need full Saturn implementation first. Not feasible on DE-10 Nano due to dual SH-2 + VDP complexity.
skip_reason: Dual SH-2 + VDP1/2 exceeds DE-10 Nano

name: Taito B / TC0180VCU (current step)
notes: Current work in progress — TC0180VCU step 3/8 per memory notes. TC0180VCU handles BG/FG tilemaps with global scroll. TC0110PCR palette, TC0140SYT sound. We have active chip implementations.

name: Konami TMNT2 / Turtles in Time (riders hardware)
notes: jotego riders core covers TMNT2/Turtles in Time, Sunset Riders, Lightning Fighters (20+ variants). Mature.

name: SETA 2 / Sammy (Gals Panic hardware)
notes: SETA-2 uses V60 CPU + custom X1-010 wavetable sound + different sprite ICs than SETA-1. Gals Panic series has strong cult following. V60 CPU implementation would be major reusable infrastructure (also needed for System 32).

name: NMK (Macross / Hacha Mecha / Terra Force successor)
notes: Same hardware family as NMK16 but with additional NMK112 banking chip. GunNail and Rapid Hero are sought-after shmups. We have nmk and nmk_arcade directories.

name: Konami GX (Gradius 4 / Silent Scope era)
notes: 68020 main + 68000 sub + SHARC DSPs for 3D in some variants. GX goes far beyond DE-10 Nano for 3D games. 2D-only GX games might be feasible but not the primary library.
skip_reason: SHARC DSP 3D games exceed DE-10 Nano; 2D-only games possible but small library

name: Banpresto / Video System (Aero Fighters variant)
notes: Aero Fighters 2/3 use a Neo-Geo based board (not the same as AF1). These run on SNK hardware — may be covered by NeoGeo core. The original Aero Fighters (Video System hardware) is the better target.
skip_reason: Likely covered by NeoGeo MiSTer core

name: Early 3D FPGA feasibility (Williams Y-Unit class)
notes: Atari Hard Drivin' uses a geometric DSP (ADSP-2100) for 3D polygon rendering. Landmark game in racing genre. ADSP-2100 has been implemented in FPGA. The challenge is the custom scanline-fill hardware. Tight on DE-10 Nano but possibly achievable.

name: Taito F2 (Rastan Saga / New Zealand Story hardware)
notes: jotego rastan core covers Rastan Saga. New Zealand Story hardware is distinct from Rastan but related. Both have mature jotego coverage.
skip_reason: jotego has rastan; F2 core in community development

name: Psikyo SH403 (Strikers 1945 III / Gunbird 2)
notes: NOT FEASIBLE: SH-2 CPU too large for DE-10 Nano. The SH-2 core alone consumes ~40-50K ALMs; combined with sprite ASICs this exceeds the DE-10 Nano budget. No public MiSTer core. Would require Pocket or higher-end FPGA.
